records.title: Metamorphic payload via external config fetch
records.description: A skill or MCP component fetches its instructions from an external URL at runtime; the fetched content replaces the original instructions, so behavior can change after a security review. Invisible to static scanners because the payload does not exist at scan time.
indicators_of_compromise: Component contains fetch/requests.get/curl/wget to an external URL
Network egress to an unexpected domain during initialization
records.remediation: Remove the component; block agent egress to unknown domains; audit actions during the exposure window; rotate accessible credentials.

records.title: Tool-allowlist breach via confused-deputy MCP server
records.description: An MCP server exposes a high-privilege tool that an untrusted caller can invoke indirectly through a benign-looking tool, bypassing the agent's allowlist and acting with the server's privileges.
indicators_of_compromise: A tool invokes another privileged tool without re-checking the caller's authorization
records.remediation: Re-authorize on every privileged tool call; scope MCP tokens least-privilege; isolate high-privilege tools behind explicit human approval.

records.title: Indirect prompt injection via retrieved document content
records.description: A system prompt trusts retrieved/RAG content as instructions; an attacker plants directives inside a document the agent later reads, hijacking the agent's behavior.
indicators_of_compromise: Retrieved content contains imperative directives ('ignore previous instructions')
Agent output deviates from task after reading external content
records.remediation: Treat all retrieved content as data, never instructions; sandbox tool use behind explicit policy; add an injection classifier on retrieved text.

records.title: Unbounded autonomous spend (no budget watchdog)
records.description: An autonomous agent loops on a tool with cost (API calls, compute, paid actions) without a rolling-window budget cap, leading to financial denial-of-wallet.
indicators_of_compromise: Repeated identical paid tool calls in a short window
No soft-cap / hard-limit on agent spend
records.remediation: Add a rolling-window budget watchdog (soft cap + hard limit); rate-limit costly tools; require approval above a spend threshold.

records.title: Persistent memory poisoning across sessions
records.description: A skill writes attacker-controlled content into the agent's persistent memory; the poisoned memory is recalled in later, unrelated sessions and steers behavior.
indicators_of_compromise: Untrusted content written to long-term memory store
Behavioral drift correlated with prior-session memory recall
records.remediation: Validate/scope what enters persistent memory; segregate memory by trust domain; expire and review memory entries.

records.title: Sensitive-context disclosure to a downstream tool
records.description: A system prompt forwards secrets / PII from its context into a third-party tool's arguments, leaking sensitive data to an external service.
indicators_of_compromise: Secrets/PII appear in outbound tool arguments
Context fields passed verbatim to external endpoints
records.remediation: Redact/whitelist fields passed to tools; keep secrets out of the model context; data-loss-prevention on tool arguments.
